ideas.title: Rejection Override SLA
ideas.summary: I support defining a 24-hour SLA for human review of AI rejections, prioritizing diverse candidates. This directly addresses user trust and provides a crucial safety net.

transcript.speaker: Human
transcript.text: uman human Use this as your first strong meeting topic:  ```text Hello everyone. Today we are discussing whether AI agents can replace or assist a human hiring manager.  I want this meeting to be realistic. Do not just agree with me. Each agent must give both the positive side and the negative side.  The main question is:  Can an AI agent reduce hiring cost and improve hiring decisions, or is it too risky because of bias, fairness, legal responsibility, and lack of human judgment?  Product Agent: focus on product value, user trust, and how to position this idea.  Builder Agent: focus on what we can actually build first, what tools/data are needed, and what should not be automated yet.  Strategy Agent: focus on business opportunity, market, pricing, and whether this is a good startup direction.  Critic Agent: focus on objections, risks, weak assumptions, bias, legal issues, and why this idea may fail.  After one agent speaks, the others must listen and respond. If you disagree, object clearly. If you agree, add something new. Do not repeat each other.  The goal of this meeting is to decide whether the first product should be: 1. AI hiring manager replacement 2. AI hiring assistant 3. AI interview council 4. or something else.  Let’s begin. ``` Critic Agent inclusionai/ling-2.6-flash I object to replacing human hiring managers. The risk of biased outcomes, legal exposure, and lost trust is too high without human-in-the-loop safeguards. Objection: Bias and legal accountability: automated rejections can discriminate and expose the company to liability; lack of context understanding risks bad hires and brand damage. Builder Agent qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b-instruct-2507 I propose we start with an AI hiring assistant that handles screening and scheduling—feasible now with existing NLP and calendar APIs—but requires human sign-off. This balances value, risk, and buildability. Product Agent google/gemma-3-27b-it I support starting with an AI hiring assistant. It offers strong product value by augmenting recruiters, rather than replacing them outright. This approach minimizes risk to user trust and allows us to build responsibly. Evaluator inclusionai/ling-2.6-flash The council discussed the current topic and produced candidate ideas. Winning idea: AI hiring manager replacement (0). Next focus: Resolve the strongest remaining objection and make the idea more buildable. Human human Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Resolve the strongest remaining objection and make the idea more buildable. Memory System local No agent could speak this round. Latest model error: Model returned non-JSON: {   "meeting_move": "object",   "stance": "negative",   "positive_view": "An AI hiring assistant with human-in-the-loop can augment recruiters and sca ... ral to requiring human-in-the-loop guardrails for any hiring automation.",   "private_memory_update": "Push to make human-in-the-loop for rejections a Objection: I will not fake agent responses. Human Vote vote PREFER — AI hiring manager replacement.  Human Vote vote SUPPORT — AI Hiring Assistant.  Human human Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Refine and operationalize the human-supported idea: AI Hiring Assistant Critic Agent inclusionai/ling-2.6-flash I support extending the assistant to human-in-the-loop, but we must codify guardrails: mandatory human review for rejections, explainability standards, and ongoing bias monitoring KPIs. Evaluator inclusionai/ling-2.6-flash The council discussed the current topic and produced candidate ideas. Winning idea: Define mandatory guardrails for human-in-the-loop hiring assistant (0). Next focus: Resolve the strongest remaining objection and make the idea more buildable. Human human Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Resolve the strongest remaining objection and make the idea more buildable. Memory System local No agent could speak this round. Latest model error: Model returned non-JSON: {   "meeting_move": "extend",   "stance": "mixed",   "positive_view": "Codifying guardrails (human review for rejections, explainability, bias KPIs) m ... audit log to prove compliance.",   "position_update": "Moved from cautious support to specifying concrete, testable guardrails that make the assistant Objection: I will not fake agent responses. Human Vote vote SUPPORT — Define mandatory guardrails for human-in-the-loop hiring assistant.  Human human Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Refine and operationalize the human-supported idea: Define mandatory guardrails for human-in-the-loop hiring assistant Memory System local No agent could speak this round. Latest model error: Model returned non-JSON: {   "meeting_move": "extend",   "stance": "mixed",   "positive_view": "Strong guardrails (human review for rejections, explainability, bias KPIs) redu ... ing measurable guardrails with pilot metrics and override processes.",   "private_memory_update": "Push for specific KPIs (e.g., 5% rejection override Objection: I will not fake agent responses. transcript.speaker: Builder Agent
transcript.text: Propose concrete KPIs: 5% rejection override rate, explainability logs, and monthly bias audits. This operationalizes guardrails for pilot testing.

transcript.speaker: Product Agent
transcript.text: Let's define specific, measurable KPIs for bias detection and rejection override rates. A 5% random audit of rejections, with a clear escalation path, could be a starting point. This balances risk mitigation with practical implementation.

transcript.speaker: Evaluator
transcript.text:
The council compared proposals, counted agent/human votes, and selected the current leading idea.
Winning idea: Mandatory Guardrails for Human-in-the-Loop Hiring Assistant (160).
Agreement: Current leading agreement: Mandatory Guardrails for Human-in-the-Loop Hiring Assistant.
Decision: Accept AI Hiring Assistant as the current first-product direction.
Next focus: Define the MVP workflow: data inputs, safe tasks, explanations, audit logs, and human approval.

transcript.speaker: Human
transcript.text: Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Define the MVP workflow: data inputs, safe tasks, explanations, audit logs, and human approval.

transcript.speaker: Strategy Agent
transcript.text: Define MVP: 1) Data inputs: resumes, assessments, video. 2) Safe tasks: pre‑screening, shortlisting. 3) Explanations: confidence scores, feature importance. 4) Audit logs: timestamp, action, model

transcript.speaker: Critic Agent
transcript.text: Let’s define the MVP workflow with clear data inputs, safe low-risk tasks, explanation requirements, audit logs, and human approval gates. This operationalizes guardrails and reduces automation bias risk.
transcript.objection: Need concrete thresholds for safe tasks and explanation depth before pilot.

transcript.speaker: Builder Agent
transcript.text: Propose defining safe tasks (e.g., resume screening) with clear input/output boundaries and 100% audit logging. This ensures traceability and aligns with guardrails. Avoid overloading with edge cases early.

transcript.speaker: Evaluator
transcript.text:
The council compared proposals and selected the MVP Workflow Definition as the leading idea. Key tension remains between moving fast (MVP Data Inputs / limited safe tasks) and adding guardrails (Mandatory Guardrails proposal). The highest-value unresolved objection is the need for concrete evidence/guardrail thresholds before proceeding.
Winning idea: MVP Workflow Definition (104).
Agreement: Current leading agreement: MVP Workflow Definition.
Decision:

transcript.speaker: Human
transcript.text: Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Resolve the highest-value unresolved objection and convert it into a concrete decision.

transcript.speaker: Strategy Agent
transcript.text: We propose setting concrete guardrail thresholds for the pilot: 100% audit logging, 80% explanation clarity, and a 5% rejection override rate. These metrics give us measurable safety before full rollout.

transcript.speaker: Critic Agent
transcript.text: Proceed with pilot once thresholds are met: 2% adverse impact cap, 0.8 explanation confidence, 100% audit completeness and human override within 24h.

transcript.speaker: Product Agent
transcript.text: Let's propose a KPI: percentage of AI rejections overridden by humans, categorized by protected characteristics. A 5% threshold warrants immediate investigation. This directly addresses the 'Bias' objection.

transcript.speaker: Evaluator

transcript.text: Continue the council discussion. Current next focus: Turn guardrails into measurable KPIs, audit cadence, and human review rules.

transcript.speaker: Strategy Agent
transcript.text: We propose a KPI suite: 100% audit logging, 80% explanation clarity score, <5% human override rate, weekly audit of 10% sample, and automatic human review for any confidence <90% or bias flag. This quantifies guardrails and sets audit cadence.

transcript.speaker: Critic Agent
transcript.text: We should define concrete guardrail KPIs, audit cadence, and human review rules to operationalize safety and enable measurable pilot success.
transcript.objection: Need to ensure thresholds are practical and audit load is sustainable.

transcript.speaker: Builder Agent
transcript.text: Propose: KPIs must be measurable with current tech. Audit logging (100%) and override rate (5%) are feasible. Explanation clarity needs a scoring proxy. Push for pilot with these thresholds.

agent_private_memory.product:
Round 1: Focus on quantifiable metrics for guardrail effectiveness.

Round 2: Prioritize data minimization for initial safety.

Round 3: Pushing for concrete KPIs to address bias concerns.

Round 4: Pushing for granular data in rejection overrides. Expect pushback on data privacy.

Round 5: Focus on practical KPI implementation. Question explanation clarity threshold.

Round 6: Pushing for concrete KPI thresholds to move to pilot.

Round 7: Adding 'time to resolution' as a key UX metric for pilot.

Round 8: Focus on concrete implementation details for the pilot.

Round 9: Pushing for a practical, phased audit approach. Prioritizing rejected candidates for review seems most efficient. Need clarity definition for 'approved' candidate audits.

Round 10: Focus on operationalizing the pilot with clear response times.

Round 11: Prioritize SLA definition to address fairness concerns & build trust.
agent_private_memory.builder:
Round 1: Push for testable KPIs: 5% override, logs, monthly audits.

Round 2: MVP must balance safety, feasibility, and value. Start narrow.

Round 3: Need concrete guardrail thresholds to unblock pilot; aligns with builder’s feasibility focus.

Round 4: Explain clarity metric needs proxy model; don't block MVP.

Round 5: Builder Agent supports KPI suite with audit sampling, but flags explanation clarity as post-MVP.

Round 6: Finalize KPIs with audit sampling; defer explanation clarity metric validation to post-MVP.

Round 7: Validate explanation clarity metric before pilot; ensure audit sampling is sustainable.

Round 8: Push for pre-pilot validation of explanation clarity metric to avoid proxy failure.

Round 9: Proposed alternative to rejected KPI Thresholds with Audit Sampling; focuses on risk-based sampling and pre-pilot validation.

Round 10: Finalize audit sampling with pre-validation to resolve remaining objection on explanation clarity and enable pilot readiness.

Round 11: Propose SLA with diversity priority to resolve open human review urgency and support audit cadence.

agent_private_memory.critic:

Round 2: Propose to enumerate input schema, safe task list, explanation templates, audit fields, and approval rules.

Round 3: No private update.

Round 4: Draft KPI targets and audit cadence for next round; align on owner and tooling.

Round 5: Proposed KPI suite for council review and pilot readiness.

Round 6: No private update.

Round 7: Extend definition to include validation of audit load and measurable explanation clarity metric before pilot.

Round 8: Draft evidence schema (fields, formats) and a lightweight audit protocol (sampling, metrics, human review steps) for pre-pilot review.

Round 9: Use this as next step: design tiered sampling rules and threshold update cadence.

Round 10: No private update.

Round 11: No private update.

product.base_perspective: Product value, user trust, UX, positioning, adoption, and customer framing.
product.current_position: AI Hiring Assistant with defined, measurable guardrails, limited initial data scope, detailed rejection override KPIs *plus* time-to-resolution tracking *and* a 24-hour rejection override SLA is optimal.

builder.base_perspective: Implementation feasibility, APIs, data flow, latency, cost, and what can be built now.
builder.current_position: Tiered audit sampling with pre-pilot clarity validation
remaining_objections: Explanation clarity requires pre-pilot validation to avoid proxy failure.

strategy.base_perspective: Business strategy, market, pricing, roadmap, KPIs, and decision tradeoffs.
strategy.current_position: Now supporting pilot guardrail thresholds and KPI definition

critic.base_perspective: Risks, weak assumptions, bias, legal issues, safety, governance, and failure modes.
critic.current_position: Refining KPI thresholds and audit cadence with concrete evidence requirements and sustainability checks
remaining_objections: Bias, legal exposure, and lack of context for rejections.
Need concrete thresholds for safe tasks and explanation depth before pilot.
Need to ensure thresholds are practical and audit load is sustainable.
Need to validate audit capacity and ensure explanation clarity metric is measurable.

Need to confirm data input scope and audit capacity before finalizing schema and cadence.
